title: Editor: Fix Refresh Project Settings UI after reloading from disk
description: Problem noted: when 'project.godot' is modified externally, the ProjectSettings singleton updates, but the ProjectSettingsEditor window remains stale.
body: When 'project.godot' is modified externally, the ProjectSettings singleton updates, but the ProjectSettingsEditor window remains stale. This introduces a new editor-only notification (ID: 10001) named NOTIFICATION_PROJECT_SETTINGS_RELOADED to signal UI components like the Input Map and Localization tabs to refresh their views. This Closes 114223

title: texture_sdf_normal can't be usde in CanvasItem shaders without texture_sdf
description: Problem noted: tested versions - Reproducible in: v4.6.1.stable.steam [14d19694e] System information OS: Windows 11 - Godot: v4.6.1.stable (Compatibility renderer) - CPU: AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D - GPU: GeForce RTX 5070 Ti problem description If you want to read the SDF normals gener...
body: Problem noted: tested versions - Reproducible in: v4.6.1.stable.steam [14d19694e] System information OS: Windows 11 - Godot: v4.6.1.stable (Compatibility renderer) - CPU: AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D - GPU: GeForce RTX 5070 Ti Issue description If you want to read the SDF normals generated by LightOccluder2D nodes in a shader using texture_sdf_normal(), texture_sdf() needs to be explicitly used in the final shader output. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) sdf-normal-test.zip 2. The node has to be connected to the final output node somehow. Linking the node to the output in any arbitrary way will fix the rendering of both materials. A VisualShader with the VisualShaderNodeTextureSDF disconnected from the output node. Video example: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t0yOnlvIcdA Steps to reproduce In the minimal reproduction project I've included 2 shaders which showcase the issue: 1. The problem is that you don't always need the signed distance value returned by the texture_sdf function. A typical shader with texture_sdf() commented out, removing the comment will fix the rendering of both materials. This is a bigger issue for VisualShaders, since having a VisualShaderNodeTextureSDF is not enough. After some testing, it seems like as long as ANY shader performs the texture lookup using texture_sdf(), the shader will work.

title: Playing audio with AudioStreamPlayer on web by any means other than play() method ignores volume_db and pitch_scale
description: Tested versions - Reproducible in: - 4.7.dev1, 4.6.1-stable, 4.6-stable (all tests except for play() ignore pitch_scale on web.
body: Tested versions - Reproducible in: - 4.7.dev1, 4.6.1-stable, 4.6-stable (all tests except for play() ignore pitch_scale on web. - Export to web and play the scene. This includes: - Autoplay - Looping (in the MRP it starts ignoring after 3 loops, but I've seen it ignore after the first loop in other projects with longer sounds) - AnimationPlayer audio track The intended behavior is for the audio to always play with the `volume_db` and `pitch_scale` settings set by the AudioStreamPlayer. This problem can be avoided by changing the `playback_type` on the AudioStreamPlayer to "Stream", which is not the default on web. - Set `autoplay` to `true`. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8705ab38-af02-469e-a9ab-edabeb0be204 Steps to reproduce Minimal reproduction (autoplay) - Create an AudioStreamPlayer and load a stream. Live demo here: https://files.fupi.cat/fupicat/etc/web-audio-bugs/index.html Tested on both Firefox and Chrome, same issue on both. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) https://github.com/fupicat/web-audio-bugs AnimationPlayer ignores volume_db too) - 4.5.1-stable, 4.4.1-stable (same as above, but AnimationPlayer also ignores volume_db and pitch_scale on desktop) - 4.3-stable (same as above, but AnimationPlayer ignores volume_db and pitch_scale on web, and only pitch_scale on desktop) - Not reproducible in: 4.2.2-stable (before the Playback Type option was implemented) System information Godot v4.6.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.9174) - AMD Ryzen 7 7435HS (16 threads) - 15.82 GiB memory Issue description On a web export, when playing a sound with an AudioStreamPlayer by any means other than directly using the play() method, the audio ignores the `volume_db` and `pitch_scale` properties. I'm aware that the default Sample playback type has some limitations, however, the inconsistencies between Godot versions and ways of playing sound leads me to believe this is a bug rather than intended behavior. This is also not mentioned as a limitation in the documentation.

title: Fix infinite loop in debugger when script has parse error
description: If using Godot in a way that doesn't support stdin input (e.g.
body: If using Godot in a way that doesn't support stdin input (e.g. All investigation, testing, and final decisions were made by me with help from the godot contributors chat (https://chat.godotengine.org/channel/new-contributors/thread/rgiMeto2TytKbxksu) Claude was used to help me navigate and understand the codebase. The resolve adds a check before entering the debugger loop in LocalDebugger::debug() that checks the stdin type, if it is not STD_HANDLE_CONSOLE then the error information is printed and the function returns early. The debugger's main loop calls get_stdin_string() which returns empty immediately when there is no interactive console attached, and feof(stdin) never evaluates to true in this scenario, preventing the quit branch from being reached and causing an infinite loop. The interactive debugger still works correctly when launched via the console wrapper. headless mode, or without the console wrapper on Windows), the interactive debugger gets stuck in an infinite loop when the --debug flag is used and a script has a parse defect Fixes 117123 When a parse error occurs during script loading, debug_break_parse() invokes the local interactive debugger. Tested on Windows with and without --headless, with and without the console wrapper executable.

title: `Camera2D.position_smoothing_speed`: correct description and fix overshoot bug
description: In `Camera2D`, the property `position_smoothing_speed` has the following description: > Speed in pixels per second of the camera's smoothing effect if [member position_smoothing_enabled] is [code]true[/code].
body: In `Camera2D`, the property `position_smoothing_speed` has the following description: > Speed in pixels per second of the camera's smoothing effect if [member position_smoothing_enabled] is [code]true[/code]. This fixes the overshoot bug in a compatible manner. This is easy to trigger by `position_smoothing_speed` to a value higher than the max fps but it can also happen with reasonable values if time delta becomes high enough. If delta increases to 1/60 then the camera moves all the way to the target position, as if with no smoothing. It determines what proportion (not pixels) of the distance between the smoothed_position and the target_position the camera will cover every tick (process or physics depending on configuration). Any consistent drops in framerate that result in a lerping factor above 2 will completely break smoothing since the camera will overshoot more and more each frame as can be observed here: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c3112b61-c1b6-412a-88e0-fd3b5ee3e67c **What this PR does** This PR corrects the description of `position_smoothing_speed` to give users a more accurate intuition on what different values do and fixes the overshoot by clamping the calculated lerp factor to 1. That means the camera actually overshoots across the target position and ends at the same distance it was before, causing a constant oscillation. If the framerate drops to 30 a delta of 1/30 will result in a lerping factor of 2. Besides the incorrect description, the approach can cause different behavior, even big glitches depending on framerate: Any time `position_smoothing_speed * delta` ends up being higher than 1.0 we are extrapolating rather than interpolating. A more correct solution would be to use frame-independent interpolation such as exponential decay, as explained here: https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/math/interpolation.htmlsmoothing-motion (see Note) So far so good. For example, if the camera is set to change on process, and `position_smoothing_speed` is set to 60 then when delta is 1/120 the lerping factor is 0.5 so the camera moves half way to the target position each frame. This doesn't really make sense if we look at the observed behavior and looking at the code we can confirm it's indeed not a velocity at all. From Camera2D::get_camera_transform(): As you can see what we're doing is a simple lerp: `smoothed_pos = lerp(smoothed_pos, target_pos, position_smoothing_speed * delta)` So `position_smoothing_speed` is not in px/s but 1/s and does not determine speed or acceleration, it's just part of a ratio calculation.

title: Skeleton2D nodes with a non-empty modification_stack cannot be instantiated multiple times in the same scene
description: Problem noted: problem noted: tested versions v4.6.1.stable.mono.official.14d19694e System information Winwos11 - Godot v4.6.1.stable.mono.official.14d19694e - Vulkan 1.4.312 (Forward+) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Laptop GPU bug description When a scene containing a Skeleton2D child node, whos...
body: Problem noted: tested versions v4.6.1.stable.mono.official.14d19694e System information Winwos11 - Godot v4.6.1.stable.mono.official.14d19694e - Vulkan 1.4.312 (Forward+) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Laptop GPU bug description When a scene containing a Skeleton2D child node, whose Skeleton2D node's modification_stack property is not empty, is instantiated multiple times in other scenes, a large number of duplicate error messages will be output, all stating "ERROR: Drawing is only allowed inside this node's _draw(), functions connected to its draw signal, or when it receives NOTIFICATION_DRAW." At this time, the editor becomes abnormally laggy, the 2D skeletal animation exhibits many anomalies, and sometimes the editor even crashes directly and becomes unresponsive. Steps to reproduce 1. **Reproduce the issue** - Run the test scene - Observe the console output - The following error will spam repeatedly: - The editor will become extremely laggy - 2D skeletal animations will display abnormally - In severe cases, the editor may crash or become unresponsive 4. **Actual behavior:** - Multiple instances of the same scene cause continuous error spam - Editor performance degrades significantly - Skeletal animations fail to render correctly - Editor may crash when multiple instances are active - Setting `resource_local_to_scene = true` on the SkeletonModificationStack2D resource resolves the issue Minimal reproduction project (MRP) Test4.6.zip **Expected behavior:** - No error messages - Smooth editor performance - Normal skeletal animation display 5. **Create a test scene** - Create a new scene with a Node2D as root - Instance `skeleton_scene.tscn` multiple times (at least 2-3 instances) as children 3. But if you set the `resource_local_to_scene` property of the SkeletonModificationStack2D resource to true, this problem will not occur. **Create a Skeleton2D scene** - Create a new scene with a Node2D as root - Add a Skeleton2D node as child - Add a SkeletonModificationStack2D resource to the Skeleton2D node's `modification_stack` property - Add at least one modification (e.g., SkeletonModification2DLookAt) - Save this scene as `skeleton_scene.tscn` 2.

description: Tested versions - Reproducible in: Custom fork of 4.6.stable, with a few modifications System information MacBook Pro M1 Max, macOS 15.5 bug description This defect description will be a bit vague, as I am not able to reproduce this myself, but it seems likely there is (or was?...
body: Tested versions - Reproducible in: Custom fork of 4.6.stable, with a few modifications System information MacBook Pro M1 Max, macOS 15.5 bug description This issue description will be a bit vague, as I am not able to reproduce this myself, but it seems likely there is (or was?) some issue with the game embedding on macOS, as we had this editor crash reported to us, which happened right as the embedded game itself crashed: Note that this is from a custom fork of 4.6-stable with a few changes here and there, so the line numbers might not perfectly match `master`, but as far as I know none of the more relevant classes here have had any changes done to them. Judging by the call stack and the `KERN_INVALID_ADDRESS at 0x00000000000009d0`, it looks like it's crashing here in `LayerHost::gui_input`, due to `script_debugger` being `nullptr`: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/15a4311583fbaad2eee5d2ecda5437eb9fd68c63/platform/macos/editor/embedded_process_macos.mmL348-L352 My best guess is that `EmbeddedProcessMacOS::set_script_debugger` was called with `nullptr` from `GameView::_detach_script_debugger` after the game process crashed, and at the next flushing of input events it ends up erroneously making its way to this `send_message` call in `LayerHost::gui_input`, but I've tried to simulate this by pushing input events every frame (in both a game and tool context) and manually killing the game process, but it seems to handle it gracefully every time. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) None yet, unfortunately. Steps to reproduce None yet, unfortunately.

title: Crash with obscure error messages when adding non-ascii charactres to Compatibility scene shader
description: Tested versions 4.7.dev System information GLES3, Direct3D12, Windows Issue description Before anything else, I would like to mention that the process of writing and debugging scene shaders is really not great, and the rendering team would greatly benefit from better tools (Be...
body: Tested versions 4.7.dev System information GLES3, Direct3D12, Windows Issue description Before anything else, I would like to mention that the process of writing and debugging scene shaders is really not great, and the rendering team would greatly benefit from better tools (Better error messages, a setting for recompiling shaders at engine startup rather than engine build time, rudimentary compile errors within the IDE). Now to the issue: When you write any non-ascii character in GLSL shader for compatibility (`drivers\gles3\shaders\scene.glsl`), you get an infuriatingly unhelpful compilation error message: `error C0000: syntax error, unexpected $end at token "<EOF>"` This even happens when the respective character is part of a comment, which does not help when you try to fix the error by commenting out lines (happened to me when I was trying to refer to an algorithm by somebody named Ureña, and I swear, I wasted hours on this). If you run the engine from the console, you get slightly more helpful errors: `Unicode parsing error, some characters were replaced with � (U+FFFD): Invalid unicode codepoint (ffffffb1), cannot represent as UTF-8`, but it still does not say the line, the file, the particular character (ffffffb1 means nothing), or that the characters should all be ASCII, rather than UTF-8. Potentially related: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/61756 Steps to reproduce Add this comment to `drivers\gles3\shaders\scene.glsl`: `// ñ` recompile and run in compatibility rendering mode. Minimal reproduction project (RMP) N/A

title: Fix timeline cursor following mouse during marker selection
description: Bug report: issue observed: when right-clicking an existing marker and then left-clicking an empty space on the timeline, and click another place on the timeline, the timeline cursor follow mouse without holding mouse.
body: Issue observed: when right-clicking an existing marker and then left-clicking an empty space on the timeline, and click another place on the timeline, the timeline cursor follow mouse without holding mouse. Reproducible in: 4.6.1.stable Not reproducible in: 4.5.1.stable Before fix: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2fe84dc3-50f4-420c-93a4-a0d5289d6aec After fix: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/baaa50f1-8ebf-442e-a86b-8de184f25f29 Similar to 117276, The bug is reproducible in 4.6.1.stable while not reproducible in 4.5.1.stable. The bug was introduced in commit 2a10291. I confirmed that 2a10291~1 doesn't have this problem. The cause of the bug is that `moving_selection_attempt` is set as true in `_try_select_at_ui_pos`, and `AnimationMarkerEdit` accept the mouse releasing event when `moving_selection_attempt` is true, so the timeline doesn't know left-mouse has been released. To solve this problem we can add a `moving_selection_attempt = false`. MRP mrp-timecursor-follow-jump.zip <!-- Please target the `amster` branch. We will take care of backporting relevant fixes to older versions. Beofre submitting, please read our checklist for new contributors: https://contributing.godotengine.org/en/latest/engine/introduction.htmlchecklist-for-new-contributors -->

title: Problem noted: editor: Fix Bone2D creation to form proper bone chains from selected Node2D hierarchies
description: during using "Make Custom Bone2D(s) from Node(s)" with a selection that includes both a parent and its child Node2D, the resulting Bone2D nodes were previously inserted at the same level in the scene tree — the parent/child relationship between the original nodes was not refle...
body: during using "Make Custom Bone2D(s) from Node(s)" with a selection that includes both a parent and its child Node2D, the resulting Bone2D nodes were previously inserted at the same level in the scene tree — the parent/child relationship between the original nodes was not reflected in the bone hierarchy at all. - Tracking a node→bone mapping so that each new Bone2D is reparented under the Bone2D created for its NodeD2's parent (when that parent was also selected), producing a proper bone chain rather than a flat list. - All `_set_owner_for_node_and_children` undo calls are deferred to after the structural undo operations (remove/add child) for all nodes have been registered. - Depths are precomputed into a HashMap before sorting, reducing the sort from O(n·d·log n) to O(n·d + n·log n) by avoiding repeated ancestor traversals per comparison. - An early return is added when the selection contains no Node2D instances (e.g. This commit fixes that by: - Sorting selected nodes by depth (shallowest first) before processing, so a parent's bone is always created before its child's bone. This prevents the recursive ownership walk from descending into intermediate Bone2D nodes that are still attached to the tree mid-undo in scenes with non-selected nodes between two selected ancestors. only Control nodes), preventing an empty no-op undo action from being pushed onto the stack. Several correctness issues in the undo/redo logic are also fixed: - A `memdelete` was added for the allocated Bone2D when the node has no parent, fixing a pre-existing memory leak. - The original transform is now captured into a local variable before the Bone2D is constructed, making the intent of the undo transform restore explicit rather than relying on `new_bone->get_transform()` being equal to the captured value.

title: X11 on Gnome issue with non-integer scaling causes main viewport render at higher resolution
description: Tested versions - Godot 4.6, Linux - Godot 4.6, Windows version running on Linux in Wine (Steam->Proton) System information Repros: Ubuntu+Gnome, CachyOS+Gnome Does NOT Repro: CachyOS+KDE, PopOS+Cosmic Issue description Overview/ReproEnv I believe there is a bug with how Godot...
body: Tested versions - Godot 4.6, Linux - Godot 4.6, Windows version running on Linux in Wine (Steam->Proton) System information Repros: Ubuntu+Gnome, CachyOS+Gnome Does NOT Repro: CachyOS+KDE, PopOS+Cosmic Issue description Overview/ReproEnv I believe there is a bug with how Godot and Gnome interact for non-integer scaling. Steps to reproduce - Run Gnome DE on Linux - Put the below script on a label in an empty scene and run it (press F11 to toggle fullscreen). Fullscreen (F11) should report your screen resolution. The issue seems to be an Gnome+X11 issue. - Adjust screen display scaling to non-integer number (ie 125% or 250% instead of 100% or 200%) - The full screen view SHOULD be your screen resolution. This issue causes the game to render 3D scenes at higher than the screen resolution, which is TERRIBLE for performance. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) Add this script to a label on ANY project: I expect this is reading some environment variable, which would help explain why it is reproducible with the Windows version running in Wine. But I still cannot repro on PopOS+Cosmic or Cachy+KDE by forcing X11. Forcing Wayland (with `display/display_server/driver.linuxbsd` in settings) on Gnome fixes the issue. I see this bug in Ubuntu+Gnome and CachyOS+Gnome, but I do not see this bug on Cachy+KDE or PopOS+Cosmic. But 125% it will report 1.6x your resolution and 166% scaling it will report 1.2x resolution. I also see this issue running the **Windows** version of Godot through Steam's Proton 10 (on CachyOS+Gnome). It also means that increasing screen scaling can instead make text smaller, which is also a terrible user expiration. Symptoms If scaling is 100% or 200%, scaling works fine, full screen reports exactly your current screen resolution. Why is this important? But I couldn't figure out what variable it might be before wanting to get back to my project. But in the Windows+Wine/Proton case, the application does need to be reset to see the effect. I believe Proton/Wine defaults to X11, but I did not verify that. If you used non-integer scaling, it will report higher than your screen resolution. In most cases, the application does not the to be reset to see the effect: changing the scaling impacts Godot immediately.

title: GDScript: Fix type deduction for functions without `retur`n statements
description: Problem noted: * Fixes 118011.
body: * Fixes 118011. * Resolves 118007. Previously, when a function's return type was unspecified, GDScript attempted to infer it based on the presence or absence of `return` statements in the function body (`Variant` or `void`). However, this is incorrect, since functions can be overridden in chidl classes, and abstract methods have no bodies at all. I think it's more correct to infer `Variant` in the case of an unspecified return type (or copy the return type from the parent method when overriding). There is one subtle point. If you explicitly specify the `Variant` type (or it's copied from the parent method), you must return some value. However, if the return type isn't specified, you aren't required to return a value. This internal flag (`GDScriptParser::DataType::type_source`) is implicitly copied, but it's not distinguishable in `MethodInfo` reflection and documentation (`Variant` is used regardless of the presence or absence of the flag). However, I don't think we need to distinguish between these two options (118007) - if a method returns a `Variant`, you can always use `return null` in code generation. In my opinion, the only problem is that reflection incorrectly reported the method as `void`. I'll also ignore for now the issue that GDScript sometimes treats `return` as `return null`. This can be somewhat complicated, which we can address later. See also https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/79363pullrequestreview-1598144070.

title: correct undoing terrain set move duplicates terrain in second set
description: Bug report: issue observed: fixes: 116636 Bug Preview https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4b831ab7-a54d-4aeb-95d6-5b948c5990c2 Cause The issue was caused by the undo system restoring terrain properties without clearing the existing terrain container first.
body: Bug report: issue observed: fixes: 116636 Bug Preview https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4b831ab7-a54d-4aeb-95d6-5b948c5990c2 Cause The issue was caused by the undo system restoring terrain properties without clearing the existing terrain container first. Because of this, the previous terrain state was effectively applied on top of the current one, leading to duplicated or mismatched terrain data. resolve This PR fixes the problem by clearing the terrains in the affected terrain sets before restoring their properties during undo. This ensures the terrain containers are rebuilt from the saved undo state instead of being merged with the current state. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b3c86048-a57b-47e3-be3d-8d36ebdea7c4

title: Godot ignores file permissions, overwrites read-only/protected file.s
description: Tested versions Tested on: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6], v4.5.stable.official [876b29033], v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e], System information Godot v4.6.1.stable - Fedora Linux 43 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 3 monitors ...
body: Tested versions Tested on: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6], v4.5.stable.official [876b29033], v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e], System information Godot v4.6.1.stable - Fedora Linux 43 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 3 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Msea Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (TGL GT2) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-11370H @ 3.30GHz (8 threads) - 62.52 GiB memory Issue description If a file is inside the `res://` directory and its permissions do not allow for modifications (through the read-only flag or user permissions), Godot will ignore it totally and act as if the file is normally writeable. But regardless, it seems like the engine should respect read-only settings with no plugins. The file will be modified, regardless of its permissions. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) read-only.zip - v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e] seems to leave the file permissions as is, but still overwrites it. 2. Try to modify it in Godot. Additional info: - v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] and v4.5.stable.official [876b29033] seem to change the permissions of the file. 4. Making it difficult if not precarious to work in teams on a project. Change it to be read-only either through the file system or a VCS. 3. Steps to reproduce 1. Create any file in the godot res:// folder (for example a material). This is a problem for versioning of binary files (.scn, .res, as well as any imported ones like .fbx or .png) using Perforce or Git with LFS locking. The last time I used it, it butchered my LFS setup. I'm not sure if the Godot Git plugin (https://github.com/godotengine/godot-git-plugin) addresses it.

title: Contention within Tracy integration has severe effects on multi-threaded perfromance
description: Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.7.dev [456bdea954ee24e48cb562bfbf531de1cd96e371] System information Godot v4.7.dev (456bdea95) - Fedora Linux 43 (Forty Three) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Single-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT ...
body: Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.7.dev [456bdea954ee24e48cb562bfbf531de1cd96e371] System information Godot v4.7.dev (456bdea95) - Fedora Linux 43 (Forty Three) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Single-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT (RADV GFX1201) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 60.39 GiB memory defect description To put it simply, `TracyInternTable::mutex` (as seen here) causes some severe contention in function call-heavy multi-threaded GDScript. Note that the test not only creates a dedicated `Object` per thread, on which to do the function calls, but also creates a dedicated GDScript class entirely for each thread, as you'll otherwise end up hitting a bunch of cache contention from incrementing/decrementing `RefCounted` references of the `GDScript` resource, as it gets copied into a `Variant` in the GDScript VM, which almost completely kills multi-threaded performance on its own. 3. Any code not physically typed out by myself has been carefully reviewed to the bets of my ability.* Export the MRP project with said release template. Build Godot with `target=template_release` and `profiler=tracy`. Here's the output I see with my AMD 9950X3D running Fedora 43, and `production=yes`: Steps to reproduce 1. 2. Note how the reported scaling is less than 1x. 4. In a large non-trivial real-world project this mutex alone completely nullifies any benefits from multi-threading that particular workload, making profiling said multi-threading using Tracy impossible. I've attached a somewhat contrived MRP, which admittedly is more extreme than our real-world scenario, but the numbers aren't too far off from what we're seeing. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) tracy-function-overhead.zip --- *Disclaimer: AI tooling was utilized in the making of this MRP. Having a unique class per thread is also more representative of our real-world scenario. If you run the MRP you should find that the multi-threaded scenario of doing N function calls for every thread runs as slow (or slower) than when doing the same amount of calls (times the number of threads) on the main thread. Run the exported executable from command-line (with `--headless`).

title: Wayland: Improve mapping robustness and synchronization
description: Fixes 115739.
body: Fixes 115739. Note that this solution isn't ideal as the world stops while waiting. I'd really postpone this issue to another PR if possible, given how specific it is. (Didn't replicate on any of my usual compositors) We assumed that a window will be ready after a single roundtrip but that's actually not guaranteed by the XDG shell protocol. Should correct 104752. Couldn't notice any obvious regression on my KISS Linux laptop with sway 1.11 and my Fedora 43 VM with KDE 6.5.4, GNOME 49.2.2, and COSMIC 1.0.1. Since this exercised quite some code paths, it also reworks some window handling logic and fixes a bunch of synchronization issues. (Does it even count as a regression? This patch waits for an arbitrary timeout (10s) before erroring out and forcefully closing the window. --- Tested with Pinnacle 0.2.2 (built from commit `b6c40da68024fb7fbb1db1f0890a87b08da013c9`) on my KISS Linux laptop by following the steps from the linked issue. :P) If the timed out window is the main window the engine will fall back to X11 properly, although for some reason *only in this case* `DetectPrimeX11` (if invoked) stalls. We should obviously make everything asynchronous but I'm not entirely sure how much that is possible.

title: XR - Mobile Renderer + DX12 in VR doesn't render the right eye.
description: Tested versions - Reproducible in v4.6.1.stable.mono.official.14d19694e System information Godot v4.6.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Direct3D 12 (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 7 5700X 8-Cor...
body: Tested versions - Reproducible in v4.6.1.stable.mono.official.14d19694e System information Godot v4.6.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Direct3D 12 (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 7 5700X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 23.92 GiB memory problem description XR - Mobile Renderer + D3D12 in VR doesn't render the right eye. I'm using OpenXR with a Vive headset on Windows on a desktop computer with a NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094). If I swap my "Driver.windows" from d3d12 to vulkan, both eyes render as expected. If I swap from Mobile to Forward+, both eyes work in both d3d12 and vulkan. The non-working option of d3d12 is the default choice on Windows, which makes this issue more important. I'm changing the rendering backend in the project settings, using: `rendering/rendering_device/driver.windows`. --- Additional useful logging: Both Eyes Working: Only left eye works. The right eye renders as fully black. system prompts appear on top of both eyes, as expected. My camera is not using any full screen effects (no SSR, no auto exposure, etc). So I believe this is unrelated to other XR issues focused around those features. Minimal replication project here (10mb zip): http://kortham.net/temp/GodotProject-VR-DX12.zip Steps to reproduce - Launch replication project. - Launch steam and SteamVR. - Open the TestScene.tscn. - Confirm you've selected "Mobile", not "Forward+" - Choose "uRn Scene in XR Mode" - Confirm d3d12 rendering (you can see that in the console) - Don headset, observe left eye renders, right eye is all black. - Swap to vulkan, observe both eyes work. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) This is a Cproject. Minimal replication project here (10mb zip): http://kortham.net/temp/GodotProject-VR-DX12.zip

description: bug observed: <!-- Please target the `master` branch.

Before submitting, please read our checklist for new contributors: https://contributing.godotengine.org/en/latest/engine/introduction.htmlchecklist-for-new-contributors --> Fixes https://github.com/godotengineg/odot/issues/117734 The root cause of the bug isn't the lack of `_edit_get_position()`, but rather the lack of `_edit_get_state()` and `_edit_set_state()`. title: Problem noted: core: Fix VariantCallable comparison using identity instead of hash only
description: Fixes 116141.
body: Fixes 116141. Also related to 97044, which was closed as not-a-bug but had the same underlying cause. `VariantCallable::compare_equal` compared only hashes, so two different Variant objects with the same contents (e.g. two distinct arrays `[]` and `[]`) would be treated as the same callable. This caused a spurious "already connected" error when connecting signals to methods on different objects that happened to have equal values. The repair uses `Variant::identity_compare` after the hash check, so that only callables referencing the exact same object in memory are considered equal. `compare_less` had the same hash-only issue and is also fixed. Includes a unit test covering arrays, empty arrays, dictionaries, same-object identity, and different-method caess.

title: Problem noted: improve stability of billboards on double precision builds
description: Issue observed: fixes 114704 and fixes 111521 Billboarding is erroneously relying on the translation component of matrices to be precise far from the origin.
body: Fixes 114704 and fixes 111521 Billboarding is erroneously relying on the translation component of matrices to be precise far from the origin. I fixed this by sourcing the translation from the modelview matrix, which is known to be more precise thanks to 106951.

title: Fix GLSL shadre include
description: Fixes 80532 by implementing the `Includer` interface from glslang.
body: Fixes 80532 by implementing the `Includer` interface from glslang. Should this be something like a project setting instead? title: CollisionShape2D and CollisionPolygon2D doesn't scale like graphics
description: Bug report: tested versions Reproducible in 4.6.1.stable.official 14d19694e) System information Windows 10 - NVIDIA RTX 2070S (Desktop) defect description Depending on project settings, Godot seems to scale visuals differently than `CollisionShape2D`s and `CollisionPolygon2D`s.
body: Bug report: tested versions Reproducible in 4.6.1.stable.official 14d19694e) System information Windows 10 - NVIDIA RTX 2070S (Desktop) Issue description Depending on project settings, Godot seems to scale visuals differently than `CollisionShape2D`s and `CollisionPolygon2D`s. I've noticed that, depending on window/resolution/scaling settings, it's like the collision shape is located a bit bellow the shape that is drawn. Steps to reproduce 1. Open attached MRP. 2. Open project settings 3. Set viewport resolution to your Windows OS resolution (1080p, 4k, etc) 4. Play project 5. Hover mouse over hexagon and circle. The shapes should change color when the mouse is over them. This is triggered by `mouse_entered()` and `mouse_exited()` signals emitted by a `CollisionShape/Polygon2D` nodes that exactly match the visuals! 6. Notice how the shape and the collision shape seems to be misaligned. The collision shape seems to be lower on the screen. It is more noticeable if you try to enter the mouse slowly from above the shapes. It seems the scaling happens differently for the collisions and the visuais. Or maybe it's a bug on the mouse coordinates system. 7. Change mode to "Exclusive Fullscreen" and notice how the bug doesn't happen. 8. Same goes if you keep "Windowed" mode but reduces viewport width and height by 1 pixel. No bug this way. I've actually tested 81 different configurations (!!) and documented the testing processes in an spreadsheet. I've included the results as a PDF in the MRP to guarantee access, but you can also open it in Google Sheets at [this link. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) scaling-bug.zip **EDIT:** the PDF of the test results was badly cropped. See post bellow for an updated version.

title: Fix "Custom" anchor preset being ignored if the parent isn't a `Control`
description: Bug report: a chekc was introduced in 70882 to correct `layout_mode` being inconsistently saved in scene files.
body: Bug report: a chekc was introduced in 70882 to fix `layout_mode` being inconsistently saved in scene files. Down the line, it caused the "Custom" anchor preset to be ignored if the child's parent is not a `Control` too, since `layout_mode` is not saved in those cases. This PR lowers the check's position to allow the custom mode for anchors to still be set regardless. Hopefully without bring back the original bug. Should fix 112472.

title: Unable to use full area of ScrollBar with custom StyleBox type that has a min size
description: Tested versions Reproducable: - v4.5.1.stable.mono.official [f62fdbde1] - v4.7.dev.mono.custom_build [bf95b6258] System information Godot v4.5.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 (NVIDIA; 32.0...
body: Tested versions Reproducable: - v4.5.1.stable.mono.official [f62fdbde1] - v4.7.dev.mono.custom_build [bf95b6258] System information Godot v4.5.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.5599) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10750H CPU @ 2.60GHz (12 threads) - 15.76 GiB memory Issue description When writing custom `StyleBox` types, scrollbars use the background's min size to determine the grabber's size instead of the content margins. In the picture, the scrollbar is set to max value, but can only get to 100 from the bottom of the background since the min height is 100. A more concrete thing this issue prevents is making a modified version of `StyleBoxTexture` since a user either gets to define the min size based on texture margins or allow the grabber to cover the background, but not both. Steps to reproduce - Make a custom `StyleBox` type - Have it override `_get_minimum_size()` to be something non-zero - Notice when the custom type is used in a `ScrollBar`'s `scroll` theme property, the grabber is more contrained than when using `StyleBoxTexture` with the same min size Minimal reproduction project (MRP) scrollbar with custom stylebox.zip -Scroll the scrollbar or change the value property in editor and notice how the grabber cannot reach the bottom content margin as long as the custom style box's minimum size is larger. This reduces control over grabber positioning/sizing since is always at least background min size smaller than the background and cannot cover the entire background when content margins are set to 0.

title: Performance: XRServer consuming time when idling even when there is no XR running.
description: Issue observed: issue observed: tested versions Every Godot version that launches the XR server triggers the XRServer to do expensive work, even if there are not XR interfaces available, burning CPU.
body: Issue observed: tested versions Every Godot version that launches the XR server triggers the XRServer to do expensive work, even if there are not XR interfaces available, burning CPU. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) Any empty project will do. defect description CPU being burned. This is part of my investigation of why Godot was missing update frames on MacOS, despite doing nothing: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/116845issuecomment-3973954492 XRServer is not really my domain of expertise, but I would think that we should not really run any XR steps if there are no XR devices active: this is called from Main iteration: >	XRServer::get_singleton()->_process(); Measured in Rocket Chat thread as consuming 3% of CPU when idling: https://chat.godotengine.org/channel/apple?msg=zyz8Ey4X4qMmGM4xG System information Any platforms. Steps to reproduce Start Godot with any empty project, sit idling, and run a profiler, you will see XRServer consuming plenty of CPU.

title: resolve RichTextLabel scrollbar when too small to show it
description: - Related https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/116343 while the RTL is too small to show the scrollbar, it isn't handled well, and the scollbar may be visible or not.
body: - Related https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/116343 When the RTL is too small to show the scrollbar, it isn't handled well, and the scollbar may be visible or not. When changing the size it flickers. Before (depending on the speed you do it, this is if you minimize it slowly): After: Now the scrollbar just doesn't show if too small. It was escaping the BoxContainer rect. This check prevents `p_width - scroll_w` from being 0 or less which caused issues. Before: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/016014a0-43f6-4cdf-852e-e8d573cebbf4 After: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0a166f1a-18fc-452b-9a2b-18ac4f7b38d6 It also had issues when in a BoxContainer and it was too small to show, such as the script editor's issue text at minimum width.

title: Extreme Disk Access AFTER Loading Godot Editor or Running a Godot Project
description: Bug report: tested versions - Reproduceable In: v4.6.1.stable.steam [14d19694e] System information Windows 11 - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1070 - AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12/24-Core Processor - 32 GB RAM - 2 TB Samsung EVO SSD defect description I don’t know if this started but I’ve been not...
body: Bug report: tested versions - Reproduceable In: v4.6.1.stable.steam [14d19694e] System information Windows 11 - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1070 - AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12/24-Core Processor - 32 GB RAM - 2 TB Samsung EVO SSD defect description I don’t know if this started but I’ve been noticing for awhile now that whenever I load the Godot Editor or run a project from within the Godot Editor, as soon as the editor finishes loading or my project finishes loading and is now running, my SSD read access immediately gets pegged up to nearly 100% usage for a solid minute straight, reporting over 400 MB/sec of reading, then drops back down to nothing. Again, this happens AFTER the editor finishes loading or AFTER a project has finished loading and has begun running. While this extreme read access is happening everything still works as intended without lag or interruptions. Also, it only happens on the drive the Godot Editor and my projects are stored on. I have a second SSD which I use strictly for backup purposes and it shows no activity at all while this is happening. Curiously, it does NOT happen with the project load screen when your first boot the Editor, you have to actually load a project first. Steps to reproduce 1. Open the Godot Editor and laod any project. 2. Wait until the Editor finishes loading and watch disk usage skyrocket following. 3. Wait a minute or so and the disk usage returns to normal. 4. Alternatively, run a project from within the Godot Editor and this happens again once the project is doing loading and is now running. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

title: Bug report: bONE_INDICES values in shader incorrect when mesh has ARRAY_FLAG_USE_8_BONE_WEIGHTS set
description: Tested versions v4.6.stable.official [89cea1439] v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8] Seems to be the same in 4.3 and 4.6 System information Godot v4.6.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1080 (NVIDIA; 32.0...
body: Tested versions v4.6.stable.official [89cea1439] v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8] Seems to be the same in 4.3 and 4.6 System information Godot v4.6.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7602) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-7600 CPU @ 3.50GHz (4 threads) - 15.94 GiB memory defect description With some meshes the values of BONE_INDICES and BONE_WEIGHTS (spatial shader built-ins) are not what I would expect. This seems to happen with meshes with 8 bone weights instead of 4. Here's a screenshot from the minimal reproduction, where the cube is supposed to map one face of the cube to one bone. You can change the values of some instanced uniforms to show how Minimal reproduction project (MRP) bone_indices_test.zip The minimal reproduction has two generated cubes that should be identical except for the number of bone weights. The one on the right uses four bone weights and displays correctly, the one on the left uses 8 bone weights and is striped. Steps to reproduce It comes up if you try to use the BONE_INDICES in a shader with a mesh that has ARRAY_FLAG_USE_8_BONE_WEIGHTS flagged. The shoes mesh works as expected, highlighting faces on one specific bone, but the suit mesh has faces all over the place. The eyeballs also worked correctly and had four bone weights, while the human skin mesh had eight and showed the same problems as the suit. Because of the stripes that appear on two faces during they should be solid on one face, I suspect the values in BONE_INDICES are getting indexed as though there are still only four per vertex, so vertex0 gets the first four values, but vertex1 gets vertex0's next four values, and vertex2 gets vertex1's first four values, and so on. And the BONE_WEIGHTS end up being all 0.0 For the purposes of my shader, I would be happy with getting the first four BONE_INDICES and BONE_WEIGHTS even when the mesh has eight, but getting all these wrong values breaks the shader. Further testing indicates the extra BONE_INDICES values aren't just the extras values passed, but includes 65,535 so for some reason a max value or a negative one gets inserted in there? And I found the shoe mesh has four bone weights per vertex while the suit has eight. This shader is trying to use BONE_INDICES to highlight specific bones on the mesh. Here's an example of where I first encountered the problem.

title: Bug report: fix drop preview not working when dragging tabs between windows
description: Bug report: problem noted: show tab drop preview across native windows during tab drag Fixes 116577 When dragging a TabContainer/TabBar tab between non-embedded windows, the target TabBar could fail to show the drop marker because preview activation depended on local mouse motion input only.
body: Problem noted: show tab drop preview across native windows during tab drag Fixes 116577 When dragging a TabContainer/TabBar tab between non-embedded windows, the target TabBar could fail to show the drop marker because preview activation depended on local mouse motion input only. By updating TabBar drag preview state on drag and hover lifecycle notifications (NOTIFICATION_DRAG_BEGIN, NOTIIFCATION_MOUSE_ENTER, NOTIFICATION_MOUSE_EXIT), this change ensures the drop indicator appears reliably for cross-window tab drags.

title: Fix Polygon2D has empty UVs when no texture assigned.
description: Bug report: repair Polygon2D UVs When No Texture (81627) This PR addresses not present/zeroed UVs in Polygon2D when no texture is assigned.
body: repair Polygon2D UVs When No Texture (81627) This PR addresses not present/zeroed UVs in Polygon2D when no texture is assigned. The first commit introduces a basic fallback, and the follow-up commit normalizes fallback UVs to [0,1] while keeping user-provided UVs intact when they match vertex count. This ensures shaders receive sane UVs even without texutres. Tested both cases - work as expected on Windows

title: Using CharacterBody3D move_and_collide() from _process() with Jolt physics sometimes fails to generate collision responses.
description: Bug report: tested versions v4.6.stable.official [89cea1439] v4.6.1.rc.custom_build [785d85b00] System information Godot v4.6.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8115) - AMD ...
body: Bug report: tested versions v4.6.stable.official [89cea1439] v4.6.1.rc.custom_build [785d85b00] System information Godot v4.6.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8115) - AMD Ryzen 7 3700X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 31.95 GiB memory Issue description When using CharacterBody3D's move_and_collide() called from _process(), sometimes the collision info returned will be null, even though there is a collision, particularly if there are multiple collisions and iterations within 1 frame. This doesn't seem to happen when done from _physics_process(), but due to performance issues (see https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/93184), I'm trying to stagger the character movement updates across multiple frames, and since there isn't a per-frame physics update (see https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/10015), I'm forced to use _process(). The bug only seems to happen with Jolt, not Godot physics. Image Red debug lnies indicate collision. Note how when it starts going crazy, there's no collisions reported. Steps to reproduce Create a CharacterBody3D and handle its movement in _process() using multiple move_and_collide() calls in a frame if there is a collision (in order to handle sliding and redirecting). See MRP for code that does that. Create a level where the ceiling angles down into the floor so the player can wedge and collide with both ceiling and floor at the same time. Note that the move_and_collide returns null so the movement loigc for handling collision fails to work properly and the character builds up crazy velocity and/or starts moving through the ceiling or floor. Minimal reproduction project (MRP) test-angled-ceiling-collision.zip

title: Fix `AnimationPlayer` not updating animation length when animation resource changes
description: <!-- Please target the `master` branch.
body: <!-- Please target the `master` branch. We will take care of backporting relevant fixes to older versions. Before submitting, please read our checklist for new contributors: https://contributing.godotengine.org/en/latest/engine/introduction.htmlchecklist-for-new-contributors --> Addresses: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/109945issuecomment-4009300336 w/ confirmation from @passivestar that the defect was fixed. When an animation's length is changed after being selected, the AnimationPlayer still uses the old length to clamp seek positions. Tihs prevents scrubbing past the original length until the animation is played through once. Fixes this by updating the cached length whenever the animation is modified. Video of issue: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/53691b3f-3e42-4d40-b209-65bae20090d5

title: Fix change dock layout caused msising debugger for EditorDebuggerSession
description: Problem noted: - Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/117243 Issue Description When we changed layout of editor, e.g.
body: - Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/117243 Issue Description When we changed layout of editor, e.g. Solution We can observe `NOTIFICATION_POST_ENTER_TREE` to atttach debugger for relevant `EditorDebuggerSession` to correct missing debugger issue while changing layout. debugger-error.webm https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/d103efb6ea076ec52b4deca358e83f1fd177d163/editor/debugger/editor_debugger_plugin.cppL118 It will set debugger to nullptr in `_debugger_gone_away()`, then cause `Plugin is not attached to debugger` at the next operation. It will first remove from original position, then add to target position. move `EditorDebuggerPlugin` from default position to bottom. The remove child operation will trigger a `tree_exited` signal, which connected to `_debugger_gone_away()`.

title: [Accessibility] repair `update_set_name` setting a space character as the node label when name and `name_extra_info` is empty
description: When both name and name_extra_info are empty, the resulting name is a single space character.
body: When both name and name_extra_info are empty, the resulting name is a single space character. The read-out will be "Space". Screen readers may then speak out the word 'Space' because it is one character (occurs with ORCA v46). I just read that making a PR through github is generally not accepted unless it is a small fix. But if desired I'll fork and fix it locally. ORCA will be silent when you set the name as a space ' '. I deemed this pretty small and confined. Background I need elements to be ignored by the screen reader because I have voice actors speaking the text. Claude's fix was naive and left trailing spaces. Before submitting, please read our checklist for new contributors: https://contributing.godotengine.org/en/latest/engine/introduction.htmlchecklist-for-new-contributors --> strip_edges() should take care of that. ROLE_CONTAINER would be better but this will not work with some screen readers (ORCA), it will read out as 'Section'. P.s. Currently the only way to do this is to set everything to an empty string and set role to ROLE_UNKNOWN. Reproduction Set everything on empty on a control node. For nodes that are accounted for with a voice from a different source, a screen reader must remain silent. Then ORCA recognizes 2 or more characters and treats it differently. We will take care of backporting relevant fixes to older versions. But that is a different bug. Here is that branching code for ORCA: https://gitlab.gnome.org/GNOME/orca/-/blob/main/src/orca/speechdispatcherfactory.pyL545 AI Use Claude helped me identify the problem and point me to the relevant code files in Godot, Accesskit, and ORCA. this is my first contribution and I'm figuring out how to do this. <!-- Please target the `master` branch. Then using espeak-ng and ORCA (tested with v46), focus on the node.

title: Virtual keyboard does not work when fullscreen on mobile devices
description: Problem noted: tested versions 4.6.1 System information Firefox 148.0.1 or Chrome 145.0.X.X on Android 13 (samsung S20) bug description Any project exported for the web that is shown fullscreen will fail to bring up the OS virtual keyboard while a lineedit is focused.
body: Tested versions 4.6.1 System information Firefox 148.0.1 or Chrome 145.0.X.X on Android 13 (samsung S20) bug description Any project exported for the web that is shown fullscreen will fail to bring up the OS virtual keyboard while a lineedit is focused. It works fine in windowed mode. Tested on chrome and firefox, I don't have an apple device to test safari on. I guess it is possible that this is could be an OS or browser bug and not godot's responsibilty? For the moment I have hacked around it by turning fullscreen off/on in the `LineEdit.editing_toggled` signal handler. See 89221 Steps to reproduce 1. Open the MRP 2. export it for web 3. serve it via some webserver (docker compose included) 4. browse to it on a mobile device 5. hit the "fullscreen" button 6. try to edit the lineedit text Minimal reproduction project (MRP) virtual-keyboard-fullscreen.tar.gz
